briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 16, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Accelerating Global Warming and Geopolitical Risks
sections.content: Recent reports warn that the pace of global warming is accelerating, approaching dangerous tipping points [144]. Geopolitical conflicts, such as the war in Iran, are teaching the European Union (EU) the lesson of abandoning fossil fuel dependence, while simultaneously undermining global climate commitments [2, 151]. Urgent responses are required for the widespread impacts caused by the climate crisis, including unusual observations such as the Earth's rotation slowing down due to climate change, leading to longer days [148, 149, 150].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Strengthening Domestic Climate Change Adaptation and Water Management Strategies
sections.content: Domestically, various adaptation efforts are underway to address climate change. In Jeju, the successful shipment of filefish, an alternative aquaculture species capable of surviving in high-temperature marine environments of 28 degrees Celsius, demonstrated climate resilience [1, 4, 18, 141]. The Korea Disease Control and Prevention Agency (KDCA) has fully activated its vector-borne mosquito surveillance system in response to climate change to protect public health, while Anseong City is enhancing agricultural adaptability by conducting training on potato cultivation techniques for climate change response [9, 10, 16]. Furthermore, the importance of establishing a water safety net is being emphasized, with forums and symposiums exploring water management solutions to combat floods and droughts in the era of climate crisis being held in Jeju and various other locations nationwide [3, 6, 7, 14]. A policy debate on heatwave and tropical night advisories suggests the need for immediate adaptation measures in urban environments [19].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Efforts and Challenges for Transitioning to a Carbon-Neutral Society
sections.content: The Republic of Korea is making multifaceted efforts to achieve its 2045 carbon neutrality goal. Chungnam Province has fully activated its '2045 Carbon Neutrality' comprehensive command center and launched its 3rd Climate Crisis Response Committee [20, 22, 24, 25, 31, 36, 59, 66], while Incheon City has designated the 15th of every month as 'Climate Action Day' to encourage citizen participation [21, 61, 69]. KB Financial Group has been selected as a 'Carbon Management Sector Honors' for nine consecutive years, leading corporate responses to climate change [11], and the Korea Institute of Geoscience and Mineral Resources (KIGAM) is actively pursuing technological approaches, such as exploring CCUS (Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage) technology as a carbon-neutral solution for national key industries [26, 37, 38, 39, 55, 63]. However, policy challenges for achieving the goal are also numerous, including criticisms of 'attempts to backtrack from the 2035 NDC (Nationally Determined Contribution)' and difficulties in public discourse on the Carbon Neutrality Act [23, 34]. Meanwhile, the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport is expected to contribute to greenhouse gas reduction in the building sector by resuming its green remodeling interest support project after three years [140].

sections.title: ⚡️ Expansion of Renewable Energy and Accelerated Infrastructure Investment
sections.content: Domestic renewable energy transition is showing significant progress, particularly in the offshore wind sector. Large-scale projects are gaining momentum as offshore wind power plants in seven regions, including Incheon, Jeonnam, Jeonbuk, and Boryeong, totaling over 11 GW, have been designated as renewable energy cluster complexes [82, 83, 84, 96, 99, 101, 106, 107, 111, 113, 114, 115, 118, 119, 121, 123, 124, 125, 126, 127, 128, 130, 131, 132, 133, 135, 136, 138]. The inflow of private capital is also active, with Hana Financial Group establishing a 500 billion won infrastructure fund to focus investments in renewable energy and AI sectors [78, 80, 88, 89, 91, 92, 97, 100, 103, 104, 109, 112, 116]. Local governments like Andong City are promoting 'energy welfare cities' that return renewable energy profits to citizens, presenting region-centric energy transition models [76, 81, 94, 98, 120]. Alongside the recognition that the existing RPS (Renewable Portfolio Standard) system alone is insufficient, the need for a transition in renewable energy deployment policies is also being raised [75], and market changes are being observed, such as the restructuring of industrial electricity tariff systems to align with renewable energy generation [86].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the climate crisis, along with various domestic and international response efforts and challenges. This is a time that requires continuous attention and action. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: The Provincial Institute of Marine and Fisheries Research has achieved its first successful demonstration of an alternative aquaculture species for climate change response. Jeju Province has successfully farmed filefish, a high-temperature adaptive species capable of surviving in sea temperatures elevated by climate change. The Jeju Ocean and Fisheries Research Institute announced on the 16th that, following a demonstration of new aquaculture species to cope with rising sea temperatures, it has successfully farmed and is now shipping filefish. The institute noted that temperatures have been steadily rising since last year... (Provided by Jeju Province)

summary.phenomenon: The Jeju Ocean and Fisheries Research Institute successfully farmed filefish, a species adaptable to sea temperatures (up to 28°C) elevated by climate change, and began shipping them on the 16th.
summary.cause: Rising sea temperatures due to climate change have made it difficult for existing aquaculture species to survive, thus highlighting the need to develop new high-temperature adaptive aquaculture species.
